description: A design proposal for the Gardener & Son studio at 2 Churchill Street. The garden connects the studio with the street and is designed to demonstrate what ecological function looks like in a commercial context -- visible, legible, and alive.

habitat.planting_method: Layered ecological planting -- stepping-stone path design

activity_log.title: Ecological Cottage Garden -- Design
activity_log.notes: A layered ecological planting designed to create the sense of moving through a small landscape. A stepping-stone path passes through the bed allowing moments of immersion. Taller shrubs and small trees form a woodland backdrop -- Elaeocarpus, Hakea, Leptospermum and Olearia establish the upper layer. Through the middle, grasses including Themeda, Poa and Dichelachne create a flowing matrix, while flowering perennials emerge through to provide cottage seasonal colour. Closer to the path, Myoporum, Dichondra, Pratia and Scaevola soften the ground plane. The bed responds to varying light conditions -- shade-tolerant shrubs behind the path, sun-loving grasses and wildflowers in the open areas.

activity_log.title: Silver Native Garden -- Design
activity_log.notes: A sunny, open planting within the lawn as a softer curved island bed. The mature pear remains dominant, with existing silverberry, agapanthus, flax and echium retained to form a light, airy backdrop in grey-blue tones. A crepe myrtle with soft lilac flower is introduced as a new structural tree, tying in with others already in the garden. Through the middle, ornamental grasses and flowering shrubs -- including a large miscanthus as focal point -- give movement and softness. Dianella, carex and euphorbia create repetition and rhythm. Toward the lawn edge, salvias, kniphofia, brachyscome and helichrysum bring seasonal colour while silver-toned and trailing plants soften the boundary between garden and grass. The reduced bed shape improves movement through the space while layered planting suppresses weeds and reduces maintenance.

activity_log.title: Silver Native Garden -- Design
activity_log.notes: A sunny, open planting redesigned as a softer curved island within the lawn. The bed has been slightly reduced in size to improve flow and create a stronger sense of openness. The mature pear remains the dominant feature at the northern rear, with the existing silverberry, agapanthus, flax and echium retained to form a light, airy grey-blue backdrop. A crepe myrtle in soft lilac is introduced as a new structural tree to connect this bed to the wider garden. Through the middle, ornamental grasses and flowering shrubs give movement and softness -- a large miscanthus forms a focal point in the lower curve, with dianella, carex and euphorbia creating repetition and rhythm. Toward the lawn edge, salvias, kniphofia, brachyscome and helichrysum bring colour through warmer months while silver-toned trailing plants soften the boundary between garden and grass.

notes: Design proposal stage -- two garden beds documented (Ecological Cottage Garden 16x8m and Silver Native Garden). Baseline score will be recorded post-installation. Full garden stocktake of all beds planned at a later date.

yield.status: Not yet eligible -- minimum score 40 required
yield.formula_note: Score x area x connectivity x verification. Formula v1.

upgrades.notes: Requires established planting and park connectivity
